basics.name: Zerun Niu
basics.label: MPhil Student in Computer Science
basics.summary: MPhil student at The University of Sydney working on federated learning, semantic communication, efficient AI systems, GenAI systems, and ML systems for resource-constrained environments.

work.name: The University of Sydney
work.summary: Tutorial delivery and student learning support for university coursework.
highlights: Delivered weekly tutorials, class activities, small-group discussions, and Q&A on course content and university learning platforms.
Supported students in developing academic communication, teamwork, and professionalism skills through guided exercises and feedback.
Created an inclusive tutorial environment to help students transition into university study and participate confidently.
Coordinated with unit staff and followed teaching guides to run tutorials consistently and effectively.

work.name: DUAL Group, The University of Sydney
work.summary: Research engineering and experimentation for distributed learning and efficient AI systems.
highlights: Built reproducible training and evaluation pipelines with configs, seeds, logging, and automated reporting.
Supported submission and revision cycles by implementing baselines, targeted ablations, and publication-quality figures and tables.
Addressed reviewer feedback through additional experiments and clarity edits in method and experiment sections.
Coordinated experiment ownership, timelines, and artifact handoff across code, plots, and tables to reduce duplicated effort.
Designed and deployed the official DUAL Group website for members, publications, and research projects.

education.score: Graduated with Distinction; final years WAM: 77/100

publications.name: Distributionally Robust Wireless Semantic Communication with Large AI Models
publications.publisher: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications

publications.name: Federated Deep Equilibrium Learning over Resource-Constrained Edge Networks
publications.publisher: Submitted to IEEE Internet of Things Journal

projects.name: Adaptive Low-Rank Optimization for Federated Learning
projects.summary: Undergraduate thesis integrating DyLoRA into a federated learning pipeline.
highlights: Reduced communication cost by 80% without loss of accuracy.

projects.name: DUAL Group Website
projects.summary: Designed and deployed the official website for the DUAL Group.
